summary.intent: Add a pinning system to the Gossamer app, allowing users to bookmark events, tool calls, and checkpoints, and view them in a dedicated Pins page organized by folder.
summary.outcome: Full pinning system implemented: PinnedEntity and PinFolder DB models, REST API endpoints, a Pins page with collapsible folder sections, and hover-reveal star pin buttons on EventItem, CheckpointMessageItem, ToolUseItem, and CheckpointCard.

repo: Project is an Electron app with a React renderer in app/src/renderer/src/ and an Express backend in src/server.ts
Prisma schema at prisma/schema.prisma; DB changes applied with npm run db:push
API client lives in app/src/renderer/src/api.ts with typed fetch wrappers
Navigation/layout managed in app/src/renderer/src/Layout.tsx; routes in app/src/renderer/src/App.tsx
Checkpoint entities use checkpointId (string), events use numeric id, tool calls use msg.uuid
PreToolUse event id is the correct identifier for tool calls in the DB

code.finding: AssistantCard's background box created a stacking context that obscured the absolutely-positioned PinButton; fixed with zIndex: 10 on the button
PinButton placed inline in the header Group row (not absolutely positioned) to avoid z-index layering issues in dense row layouts
PinnedEntity has a @@unique([entityId, entityType]) constraint to prevent duplicate pins, and folderId is nullable so pins don't need to belong to a folder

workflow: Server process was not caught by pkill; needed to identify and kill by port (lsof/kill on port 3000) before restarting
Prisma DB was already in sync when db:push was run at the end — schema had been applied earlier in the session
Background task notifications appeared mid-conversation but required no action since server status had already been verified

friction: Pin button not appearing on AssistantCard replies due to z-index stacking issue from background box — required a second fix pass
Server restart failed with pkill and required a port-based kill approach
User interrupted mid-response when reporting the AssistantCard bug, requiring re-read of components to re-establish context
open_items: Folder assignment UI deferred — users cannot yet move pins into folders from the UI (only via direct API calls)
Pins page renders tool_call entities using msg.uuid but server resolves them from CheckpointMessage table — may need validation that uuid lookups are consistent
No optimistic UI update on pin toggle — PinButton manages local state but Pins page would need a refetch to reflect changes
entityType values are untyped strings ('event' | 'tool_call' | 'checkpoint') — could benefit from an enum in both Prisma schema and TypeScript
No pagination or search on the Pins page
